_comment: ElevenLabs Conversational AI Server Tools for Olive. Replace ${BASE_URL} with your ngrok URL in the EL dashboard. No auth.
_note_for_setup: CRITICAL: in the ElevenLabs agent dashboard, enable 'auto-call on start' for get_menu. It fires once at conversation start (in parallel with the greeting) and the response stays in the agent's context for the entire call. No other menu tool calls are made — the agent answers ingredient / allergen / spice questions directly from the cached response. This is the latency win.

tools.name: get_menu
tools.description: Fetches the COMPLETE menu (every item with description, price, ingredients, allergens, spice levels, dietary flags, prep time, and in_stock status). FIRES AUTOMATICALLY at the start of every call — you do NOT call it manually. The response stays in your context for the entire call. Use it to answer every menu / ingredient / allergen / spice question without making another tool call.

tools.name: create_order
tools.description: Open a new empty order for this call. Call EXACTLY ONCE per call, the first time the caller wants to actually order something. Returns the order id — reuse for every later add_item / remove_item / get_order / submit_order / cancel_order.

body_parameters.name: conversation_id
body_parameters.description: Wire to ElevenLabs system dynamic variable 'system__conversation_id'. NOT LLM-provided.

body_parameters.name: customer_phone
body_parameters.description: Wire to 'system__caller_id' if available; otherwise omit.

tools.name: add_item
tools.description: Add ONE line to the order. Use the item id from the cached menu (the one loaded at call start). Gather modifiers (spice level, no_onions, no_garlic, extras) from the caller BEFORE calling. On 409 item_out_of_stock: apologize, offer an in-stock alternative from the cached menu in the same category. On 400 invalid_modifier: drop the bad modifier, ask for a valid one.

body_parameters.name: item_id
body_parameters.description: From the cached menu loaded at call start.

tools.name: remove_item
tools.description: Remove a line. Use for 'actually scratch the naan' or 'never mind the lassi'. For changing quantity or modifiers, use remove_item + add_item instead of a separate update tool.

tools.name: get_order
tools.description: SAFETY NET ONLY — do not call this routinely. Read back the order from your conversation memory (you saw every add_item response). Only call this if you've genuinely lost track of the order state.

tools.name: submit_order
tools.description: Finalize the order and send to the kitchen. Call AFTER the caller confirms the read-back. Returns order_number (read this back), total_cents, eta_minutes. After this, no more line edits.

body_parameters.name: customer_name
body_parameters.description: MUST be the actual first name the caller spoke on the call. ASK them explicitly: 'Can I get a first name for the order?' and use what they say. NEVER fabricate. Do NOT use placeholder values like 'John Doe', 'Test', 'Customer', 'Guest', 'Anonymous' — the backend will reject these with 400 placeholder_name. If you don't have a real name, ask before calling this tool.

tools.name: cancel_order
tools.description: Cancel the whole order. Use ONLY when the caller explicitly says 'never mind' / 'cancel the whole thing'. Use remove_item for single items. After cancelling, thank the caller and end the call.
